agent_outputs.raw: The pilot has three confounds that prevent causal inference. Self-selection of large sophisticated merchants, concurrent authentication changes, and approved-only label observability with different approval policies. All three need to be controlled before network-wide expansion is justified. Critique wins.

Three structural problems make the pilot evidence insufficient: merchant self-selection, concurrent authentication as a competing treatment, and fraud rate incomparability across merchants with different approval thresholds. The same-calendar-week design controls only for timing. A randomized within-merchant test with authentication held constant is required. Critique wins.

The pilot result is not causally clean. Large sophisticated merchants self-selected; some adopted new authentication during the pilot; fraud labels are only observable for approved transactions, and approval thresholds differed across groups. Network-wide expansion requires a properly controlled experiment. Critique wins.

synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins. All three agents converge on the same three confounds. The pilot is suggestive but not causally sufficient for the expansion recommendation.

etd_constraint.condition: Within comparable merchants randomized test with stable authentication and consistent approval outcome tracking; difference-in-differences using pre-pilot data
etd_constraint.supports_critique_if: Controlled test shows fraud improvement shrinks materially after removing merchant characteristic and authentication confounds
etd_constraint.supports_defense_if: Within comparable stable-auth merchants, model shows significant fraud reduction versus matched controls
etd_constraint.ambiguous_if: Some model contribution but magnitude unclear due to residual confounding from merchant heterogeneity
